diff -Nur '--exclude=.git' '--exclude=_build.*' '--exclude=*.pyc' '--exclude=__pycache__' /home/adrian/opencode/pbuild-ai-examples/results/generate-new-package/20260630_145705/source_backup/ripgrep/ripgrep.spec /home/adrian/opencode/pbuild-ai-examples/sources/ripgrep/ripgrep/ripgrep.spec
--- /home/adrian/opencode/pbuild-ai-examples/results/generate-new-package/20260630_145705/source_backup/ripgrep/ripgrep.spec	1970-01-01 01:00:00.000000000 +0100
+++ /home/adrian/opencode/pbuild-ai-examples/sources/ripgrep/ripgrep/ripgrep.spec	2026-06-30 15:12:06.207503301 +0200
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+# | name:         ripgrep
+# | summary:    A line-oriented search tool that recursively searches for a regex pattern
+# | maintainer: packager
+# | license:      MIT AND Unlicense
+# | upstream_url: https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep
+
+Name:           ripgrep
+Version:        15.1.0
+Release:        0
+Summary:        A line-oriented search tool that recursively searches for a regex pattern
+License:        MIT AND Unlicense
+Url:            https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep
+Source:         https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/archive/refs/tags/%{version}.tar.gz#/ripgrep-%{version}.tar.gz
+BuildRequires:  cargo
+BuildRequires:  rustup
+
+%description
+ripgrep (rg) recursively searches your current directory for a regex pattern while
+respecting gitignore rules. By default, ripgrep will skip hidden files/directories
+and binary files. It is fast because it is written in Rust.
+
+%prep
+%autosetup -n %{name}-%{version}
+
+%build
+export CARGO_HOME=%{_builddir}/cargo-home
+rustup-init -y --no-modify-path --default-toolchain stable 2>/dev/null || true
+PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" \
+    cargo build --release %{?_smp_mflags}
+
+%install
+export CARGO_HOME=%{_builddir}/cargo-home
+install -Dm0755 %{_builddir}/target/release/rg %{buildroot}%{_bindir}/rg
+
+%files
+%{_bindir}/rg
+
+%fdupes %{_datadir}
